title: QA: lsp.rs uses magic 500ms sleep for server warm-up (race-prone)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/tools/lsp.rs:190-191
## Violation
After sending `textDocument/didOpen`, the code sleeps a fixed 500ms then sends the real request. There is no synchronization with the server — slow machines, large files, or cold-started rust-analyzer (which can take 30+ seconds to index) will receive the request before they've finished processing, producing empty/wrong answers. Fast machines pay the latency tax unnecessarily.

Quoted (lsp.rs:190-191):
```
    // Give server a moment to process the file
    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(500));
```

Comments like "give server a moment" are a tell that the contract was never specified.
## Mandated refactor
1. Use LSP's progress reporting (`$/progress` with `WorkDoneProgressEnd`) to detect when indexing completes, OR
2. Issue the request immediately and retry on the server's well-known "content modified" / empty response codes, OR
3. At minimum, make the timeout configurable per-language (rust-analyzer needs MUCH more than 500ms for any non-trivial project).
## Filed from QA review dispatch
